name: Let Go
text: Action: Choose an attachment – discard it.

versions.flavor: The material world is but a distraction.
The Dragon's strike was not meant to kill, but to teach — and in that single moment of contact, his enemy's sword learned the lesson of impermanence.

name: Ornate Fan
text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)

versions.flavor: At times, the ornamentation is the point. At others, it is merely a distraction.

“There is another kind of artist, who sets more soberly about his work, striving to give real beauty to the things which men actually use and to give to them the shape which tradition has ordained.” 
— Murasaki Shikibu

name: Pilgrimage
text: During conflicts at this province, cancel all ring effects. (If this province breaks, resolve ring effects as normal.)

versions.flavor:
“Faith is the greatest armor. The Seppun understand this as they guard the Empress — and as they brave the mountain's elemental trials.”
— Teachings of the Imperial Guard

name: Adopted Kin
text: Fire role only. Limit 1 per character. Each other attachment on attached character gains ancestral.

versions.flavor: Facing a crisis of children, the Dragon were willing to turn even to the peasantry to find new heirs.

name: Fine Katana
text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)

versions.flavor:
Immortal steel waits
Venerated divine blade
The fires of the soul

“Just as a horse must have endurance and no defects, so it is with weapons. Horses should walk strongly, and swords and companion swords should cut strongly.”
— Miyamoto Musashi

name: Niten Adept
text: Conflict Action: If you control an attachment on this character, choose a participating character without attachments - bow that character.

versions.flavor: Two swords danced as one, the high cut drawing the parry while the low strike found flesh beneath raised steel.

name: Stoic Rival
text: Rally. (After this card is revealed in a province, add the top card of your dynasty deck to the province, faceup.) Action: During a conflict in which this character is participating, choose another participating character with fewer attachments than this character - dishonor that character.

name: Jade Tetsubō
text: Attach to a character you control. Restricted. Action: While attached character is participating in a conflict, bow this attachment. Choose a participating character with lower [conflict-military] skill than attached character – return all fate on that character to its owner's fate pool.

name: Keen Warrior
text: Sincerity. Reaction: After you look at 1 or more cards in an opponent's hand (or those are revealed) - draw 2 cards, then place 1 card from your hand on the bottom of your conflict deck.

name: Niten Master
text: Reaction: After you attach a Weapon attachment to this character – ready this character. (Limit twice per round.)

name: Solitary Hero
text: This character cannot be evaded by the covert keyword. Action: During a conflict in which this character is participating on your side alone - remove 1 fate from each other participating character with equal or lower [conflict-military] skill.

name: Daimyō's Favor
text: Attach to a character you control. Action: Bow this attachment – reduce the cost of the next attachment you play on this character this phase by 1.

name: Elegant Tessen
text: Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.) Reaction: After this attachments enters play, if it is on a character with printed cost 2 or less - ready attached character.

name: Finger of Jade
text: Attach to a character you control. Interrupt: When the effects of a card ability that targets attached character would initiate, sacrifice this attachment – cancel those effects.

versions.flavor: “The tears of Amaterasu, crystallized into protection against the darkness that seeps from Jigoku.”

name: Seeker of Fire
text: You may replace 1 province of any element with an additional [element-fire] province while deckbuilding. Reaction: After a [element-fire] province you control is revealed – gain 1 fate.

name: Togashi Yokuni
text: Action: Choose a triggered ability printed on another character – this character gains that ability until the end of the phase. (Max 1 per round.)

versions.flavor: "What transpires in our mountains is a mere pebble against the avalanche that is coming."
versions.illustrator: Shawn Ignatius Tan and Le Vuong

name: Agasha Sumiko
text: Interrupt: When this character leaves play, choose an opponent - if that player is more honorable than you, they lose 2 honor. If that player has more fate than you, they lose 2 fate. If that players has more cards in hand than you, they discard 2 cards from their hand.

name: Ancestral Lands
text: This province gets +5 strength during [conflict-political] conflicts.

versions.flavor: "Our claim on these lands stretches back to the dawn of the Empire."
“We cannot know if the sun has set forever on the Emerald Empire, but its remembered glory will shine through our long nights of exile.”

name: Doomed Shugenja
text: Limited. (No more than one limited card can be played by each player each round.) You cannot place fate on this character when it is played from one of your provinces.

name: Inscribed Tantō
text: While you have claimed the [element-void] ring, attached character is immune to opponents' ring effects.

versions.flavor: "Words of wisdom are nice, but sometimes you just need a sharpened claw."

name: Kitsuki Yuikimi
text: Reaction: After you gain a fate from a ring during a conflict in which this character is participating - this character cannot be chosen as the target of your opponent's triggered abilities until the end of the conflict.

name: Ancestral Daishō
text: Ancestral. (When attached card leaves play, return this card to your hand.) Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)

name: Kitsuki's Method
text: Ancestral. (When attached card leaves play, return this card to your hand.) Restricted. (No more than two restricted attachments per character.)

versions.flavor: Every detail, no matter how small, carries its own implications of the truth.

name: Manicured Garden
text: Conflict Action: Gain 1 fate.

versions.flavor: The perfect garden maintains all five elements in harmony.
“In equilibrium, beauty is revealed.”

name: Tetsubō of Blood
text:
This card's fate cost can only be paid for with fate on characters you control.
Limited. Restricted.
Attached character cannot be honored.

name: Agasha Swordsmith
text: Action: Search the top 5 cards of your conflict deck for an attachment, reveal it, and add it to your hand. Shuffle.

versions.flavor: “Other smiths forge with strength alone, but the Agasha way teaches patience and precision. We do not simply make swords — we awaken the potential sleeping within the metal.”

name: Hiruma Skirmisher
text: Reaction: After you play this character – it gains covert until the end of the phase.

versions.flavor: "My lord, I bring grave tidings from beyond the Wall."

name: Illustrious Forge
text: Reaction: After this province is revealed - search the top 5 cards of your conflict deck for an attachment and put it into play attached to a character you control. Shuffle.

name: Kitsuki Counselor
text: Composure - This character gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political]. (You have composure if your honor bid is lower than an opponent's.)

versions.flavor: "What is there to learn from a book that cannot be taught by a flower?"

name: Mirumoto Raitsugu
text: Action: While this character is participating in a conflict, choose a participating character controlled by your opponent – challenge that character to a [conflict-military] duel. If the loser of the duel has no fate on it, discard it. Otherwise remove 1 fate from it.

name: Tattooed Wanderer
text: You may play this character as an attachment with the text: "Attached character gains covert."

versions.flavor: It is said that Dragon shugenja and courtiers are warriors, their warriors are monks, and their monks are inexplicable.

name: Inventive Mirumoto
text: Action: If you have claimed the [element-water] ring, choose an attachment in your discard pile - play that attachment on this character (paying all costs).

versions.flavor: "When you understand the path of the sword, your adversary can do nothing you cannot forsee." - Mirumoto's Niten

name: Pathfinder's Blade
text: Seeker role only. Interrupt: When the effects of a triggered ability on the attacked province would initiate, if attached character is attacking, sacrifice this attachment – cancel those effects.

name: Riot in the Streets
text: Action: During a conflict at this province, if you control 3 or more participating Bushi characters, choose a participating character – bow that character.

name: Iron Mountain Castle
text: Each [clan-dragon] character you control can have an additional restricted attachment. Interrupt: When you play an attachment on a character you control, bow this stronghold - reduce the cost to play that attachment by 1.

name: Mountaintop Statuary
text: Reaction: After this holding is turned faceup – move it onto your stronghold province. Action: During a conflict at this province, sacrifice this holding. Choose an attacking character with printed cost 2 or lower – move that character home.

name: Alchemical Laboratory
text: While you have claimed the [element-fire] ring, each attachment you control on another player's character gains ancestral.

versions.flavor: "I have seen no interest among the fire kami for keihi bark - except when ground to paste, which seems to bring them great delight." - from the journals of Agasha Shunsen

name: Two-Heavens Technique
text: Attach to a Bushi character. While there are exactly 2 Weapon attachments on attached character, attached character gains covert.

versions.flavor:
"One man, one sword, one strike." - Kakita
 "Two hands." - Mirumoto
